question.text: What proportion of independent directors is required on the board of a Credit Rating Agency if the board is chaired by an executive director?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per SEBI circular, if the board of the CRA is chaired by an executive director, half of the board shall comprise of independent directors. If the board is chaired by a non-executive director, one-third of the board shall comprise of independent directors. **The Trap:** One-third is the requirement when the board is chaired by a non-executive director -- the question specifically asks about an executive director as chair. **Cross-Exam Context:** Corporate governance requirements for CRAs are tested in NISM Series III-A and related exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The higher proportion of independent directors when the chair is an executive director provides an additional check on management influence in governance.
